name: Black Pope
description: The term "Black Pope" is a controversial and often conspiratorial term used to refer to the Superior General of the Society of Jesus (Jesuits). The theory posits that the Black Pope wields significant, even greater, power than the actual Pope (the "White Pope"). Proponents claim the Black Pope secretly controls Vatican policy and global affairs through the Jesuit order's vast network and influence in education, politics, and finance. The origin of the term is debated, with some linking it to the historical black robes worn by Jesuits or to perceptions of them as shadowy figures. Evidence cited by proponents often includes the Jesuits' historical involvement in political intrigue, their alleged oaths of secrecy, and interpretations of their global reach as evidence of undue influence. Mainstream historians and Vatican scholars generally dismiss the "Black Pope" theory as a baseless conspiracy. They argue that while the Superior General of the Jesuits holds an important position within the Catholic Church, their authority is subordinate to the Pope. They emphasize that the Society of Jesus is subject to papal authority and that the idea of a hidden power structure is a misrepresentation of the order's role and function. Furthermore, critics point out the lack of concrete evidence to support claims of secret control and highlight the historical anti-Jesuit sentiment that has fueled such theories.
subtitle: Superior General of the Jesuits Conspiracy

claims.claim: The Black Pope secretly controls the Vatican.
claims.evidence: Jesuits' historical influence and global network.
claims.criticism: Lack of verifiable evidence; Jesuits are subordinate to the Pope.

claims.claim: The Black Pope is more powerful than the White Pope.
claims.evidence: Jesuits' alleged expertise and involvement in global affairs.
claims.criticism: Jesuits are ultimately subject to papal authority; power dynamics misrepresented.

claims.claim: The Jesuits have a secret agenda to control the world.
claims.evidence: Historical accusations of political manipulation and perceived oaths of secrecy.
claims.criticism: Conspiracy theory unsupported by credible evidence; misinterpretation of Jesuit activities.

keyFigures.name: Alberto Rivera
keyFigures.role: Former Jesuit priest who promoted anti-Jesuit conspiracy theories.

keyFigures.name: Malachi Martin
keyFigures.role: Former Jesuit and author who wrote about alleged Vatican conspiracies.

keyFigures.name: John W. O'Malley
keyFigures.role: Jesuit historian who provides academic analysis of the Society of Jesus.

timeline.event: Founding of the Society of Jesus by Ignatius of Loyola.
Suppression of the Society of Jesus by Pope Clement XIV.
Restoration of the Society of Jesus by Pope Pius VII.
